asset_name: Timing Bar and Judgement Strip — perfect, good, miss, and combo states
shared_properties: The strip centers near the bottom of the screen and stays readable while the player watches the incoming lane.
Perfect and good windows rely on strong cyan and lime contrast against the dark bar body.
The widget must support repeated fast judgments without visual smearing.
differences: This task covers the HUD timing widget instead of the world-space lane art.
Projectile bodies and telegraph markers are handled by obstacle tasks.

reference_images.label: reflect_academy_moodboard
reference_images.purpose: Moodboard for balancing bright judgment cues against the dark academy interface palette.

reference_images.label: reflect_academy_player_silhouette
reference_images.purpose: Player silhouette board for preserving clear line-of-sight between the lower UI strip and the student pose.

bevy_constraints.asset_loading: Load assets/art/reflect_academy/trial_2/ui_timing_bar_judgement_strip.png during DuelLoading and cache the atlas under ui_timing_bar_judgement_strip.
runtime_notes: Use four atlas states named idle, good, perfect, and miss.
Anchor the widget to the overlay camera at x=960 y=860 and keep the judgment marker centered.
Drive the state swaps from timing results while the moving tick indicator is rendered as a separate runtime sprite.

context.usage: Primary player-facing timing strip for reflection input quality and combo confirmation.

timing_windows.miss_behavior: falls back to red flash and empty center marker

shape.silhouette: Long beveled timing strip with central hit marker, inner target band, and judgement flare caps.

acceptance_criteria: State sheet is exactly 3600x64px with four 900x64 states and no padding.
Perfect and good windows are visually separable at first glance through both width and hue.
The widget centers cleanly at x=960 y=860 and leaves the card hand visible below it.
Miss state clearly communicates failure without obscuring the incoming projectile path.
